summary: Justin shares his personal journey to the first multifamily deal, emphasizing partnership strategy to access capital and lending ability while leveraging operational expertise. He advocates finding complementary partners based on skill gaps rather than trying to do everything alone, and demonstrates this with his $100k condo first deal in 2010 that achieved 13% cash-on-cash and 25% annualized return.
takeaways: Start small with your first deal—Justin's first was a $100,000 condo with $25,000 down payment
Achieve 13% cash-on-cash return and 25% annualized return targets on value-add deals
Partner based on complementary skill sets: if you have operations/deal-sourcing, find a capital/lending partner; if you have capital, find an operations partner
50/50 partnership split can work effectively when both parties bring non-negotiable value
Build momentum through graduated property sizes: single-family → 2-4 units → 5-10 units → larger complexes
Use broker commissions as sweat equity to roll into deals
Focus on your best skills and hire/partner for weaknesses rather than trying to master everything

skill_candidates.change: Validate deal underwriting targets of 13%+ cash-on-cash and 25%+ annualized returns before committing capital
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin cashed the $100k condo at 13% cash-on-cash with 25% annualized return; he emphasizes underwriting as a core skill he brought to partnership

skill_candidates.change: Identify your complementary skill gap (capital/lending vs. operations) and actively recruit a partner with that capability before sourcing first deal
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin spent 3 years (2006-2009) identifying his gaps, then deliberately recruited Christopher Polly based on specific capability needs; this partnership model launched their company in 2009 and first deal in 2010

title: Lesson 1 - First Deal
